title: coprosma dentata
intro: Coprosma dentata, also known as the New Zealand button fern or mirror bush, is a small, shrubby plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its glossy, oval-shaped leaves and small white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, and it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves of the plant have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and fight infection. Coprosma dentata is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
